concepts.title: Beats Frequency
concepts.conceptBody:
Beats

When two sound waves of nearly equal (but not exactly equal) frequencies travel in same direction, at a given point due to their superposition, intensity alternately increases and decreases periodically. This periodic waxing and waning of sound at a given position is called beats.

Calculation of beat frequency 

Suppose two waves of frequencies ${{f}_{1}}$ and ${{f}_{2}}\left( <{{f}_{1}} \right)$ are meeting at some point in space. Let the oscillations at some point in space $\left( {} \right.$ say $x=0$ ) due to two waves be ${{y}_{1}}={{A}_{1}}\text{sin}2\pi {{f}_{1}}t$,

${{\text{y}}_{2}}={{\text{A}}_{2}}\text{sin}2\pi {{\text{f}}_{2}}\text{t}$

If they are in phase at some time ${{t}_{s}}$ then

$2\pi {{f}_{1}}t=2\pi {{f}_{2}}t\text{ or }{{f}_{1}}t={{f}_{2}}t$

They will be again in phase at time $\left( t+T \right)$,

$2\pi {{f}_{1}}\left( t+T \right)=2\pi {{f}_{2}}\left( t+T \right)+2\pi$

$\Rightarrow {{f}_{1}}\left( t+T \right)={{f}_{2}}\left( t+T \right)+1$

$\Rightarrow T=\frac{1}{{{f}_{1}}-{{f}_{2}}}$

where $T$ is the time period of the beat or Beat frequency $f={{f}_{1}}-{{f}_{2}}$

Note : If the waves are in phase at some time $\left( t=0 \right)$ will be constructive and the resultant amplitude will be ${{A}_{1}}+{{A}_{2}}$, where ${{A}_{1}}$ and ${{A}_{2}}$ are the amplitudes of individual sound waves.

 

But at some time $\left( t={{t}_{0}} \right)$ because the frequencies are different, the waves will be out of phase or the interference will be destructive and resultant amplitude will be ${{A}_{1}}-{{A}_{2}}$.

And $\text{T}=2{{\text{t}}_{0}}=\frac{1}{{{\text{f}}_{1}}-{{\text{f}}_{2}}}$

Where ${{t}_{0}}$ is the time between constructive and destructive interference.

Alternative Method: 

${{y}_{1}}=A\text{sin}\left( 2\pi {{f}_{1}}t \right),{{y}_{2}}=A\text{sin}2\pi {{f}_{2}}t$

Now as $\text{sinC}+\text{sinD}=2\text{sin}\left( \frac{\text{C}+\text{D}}{2} \right)\text{cos}\left( \frac{\text{C}-\text{D}}{2} \right)$

$y=2A\text{cos}2\pi \frac{\left( {{f}_{1}}-{{f}_{2}} \right)t}{2}\text{sin}2\pi \frac{\left( {{f}_{1}}+{{f}_{2}} \right)t}{2}$

or $y=2A\text{cos}2\pi {{f}_{A}}t\text{sin}2\pi {{f}_{av}}t$ with ${{f}_{A}}=\frac{{{f}_{1}}\sim {{f}_{2}}}{2}$ and ${{f}_{av}}=\frac{{{f}_{1}}+{{f}_{2}}}{2}$

or $y={{A}_{b}}\text{sin}2\pi {{f}_{av}}t$ with ${{A}_{b}}=2A\text{cos}\left( 2\pi {{f}_{A}}t \right)$

Thus, the resultant wave is a harmonic progressive wave of frequency ${{\text{f}}_{\text{av}}}$ i.e., $\frac{{{\text{f}}_{1}}+{{\text{f}}_{2}}}{2}$ and amplitude ${{A}_{b}}$ which is periodic in time.

Also, it can be seen that a beat, that is maximum and minimum intensity, will occur when

$\text{I}\propto \text{A}_{\text{b}}^{2}=\text{max}($ or $\text{min})$

or $\text{cos}2\pi {{\text{f}}_{\text{A}}}\text{t}=\pm 1$ or zero

or $2\pi {{\text{f}}_{\text{A}}}\text{t}=0,\pi ,2\pi \ldots$ or $\pi /2,3\pi /2,5\pi /2$

i.e. $\text{t}=0,\frac{1}{2{{\text{f}}_{\text{A}}}},\frac{2}{2{{\text{f}}_{\text{A}}}},\frac{3}{2{{\text{f}}_{\text{A}}}}$ or $\frac{1}{4{{\text{f}}_{\text{A}}}},\frac{3}{4{{\text{f}}_{\text{A}}}},\frac{5}{4{{\text{f}}_{\text{A}}}}$

$\Delta\text{ t}=\frac{1}{2{{\text{f}}_{\text{A}}}}$ beat frequency $={{\text{f}}_{\text{b}}}=\frac{1}{\Delta\text{ t}}={{\text{f}}_{1}}\sim {{\text{f}}_{2}}$
